title: pycnandra micrantha
intro: Pycnandra micrantha, also known as the Kava of Vanuatu, is a plant species native to the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu. It is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Sarcolaenaceae, and its roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal and cultural significance. The roots of Pycnandra micrantha contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic properties, which may help to reduce anxiety, stress, and inflammation, and provide various health benefits such as improved sleep and reduced pain. In traditional medicine, the roots are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to prepare teas, infusions, or other herbal preparations for medicinal purposes.
